title: Beyond the Riviera: Northern Albania Beaches by Car Guide
excerpt: Discover sandy, family‑friendly beaches north of the Riviera. Pick up a car at TIA airport and explore Shëngjin, Velipojë, Lezhë and hidden dunes in just a few…
intro: If you’re landing at Tirana Rinas (TIA) and want to escape the crowded Riviera, the northern coast offers long stretches of soft sand, shallow waters and plenty of space for a relaxed road trip. With a rental car you can reach the hidden gems of Shëngjin, Velipojë and Lezhë in under two hours, turning a simple drop‑off into a day of seaside discovery.

sections.title: Northern Albania Beaches by Car: Shëngjin, Velipojë & Lezhë
sections.body: The northern coastline is a contrast to the rocky coves of the south. Shëngjin opens onto a wide, golden beach backed by a promenade and clear, shallow water—perfect for families. Just a short drive north, Velipojë sits at the mouth of the Buna River delta, where the sand is famed for its high iodine content and soft texture. Further inland, the Lezhë area offers secluded stretches within the Kunë‑Vain‑Tale Reserve. All three spots are easily reachable by car, making northern albania beaches by car a practical choice for a day‑trip.

sections.title: Rana e Hedhun: The Thrown Sand Dune
sections.body: Rana e Hedhun, literally ‘Thrown Sand’, is a striking coastal dune near Shëngjin. The site is inaccessible by public buses, so a private vehicle or taxi is required. Once you arrive, you can walk the dune’s crest for panoramic views of the Adriatic and the surrounding lagoons. The sand’s fine texture and the gentle slope make it a unique photo spot, especially at sunrise. Because the dune is off the main tourist trail, arriving by car ensures you avoid the crowds and enjoy a quiet moment by the sea.

sections.title: Driving the Coastal Route: From TIA to Shëngjin
sections.body: After picking up your vehicle from AutoConnect Rental at TIA, follow the A1 highway north toward Durrës, then switch to the SH1 coastal road. In about 60‑90 minutes you’ll cross the historic Lezhë Bridge and arrive at Shëngjin. The drive offers occasional stops at medieval castles, small fishing villages and the expansive Kunë‑Vain‑Tale wetlands. Keep an eye on the road signs for the beach turn‑offs; the route is well‑marked and the scenery makes the short journey feel like a mini‑adventure.

sections.title: Family‑Friendly Spots: Velipojë and Lezhë
sections.body: Velipojë’s long, sandy shoreline is ideal for kids who love to build sandcastles and paddle in the gentle surf. The nearby river delta creates shallow lagoons that calm the waves further, providing a safe environment for younger swimmers. In Lezhë, the reserve’s hidden bays are reachable via gravel roads that only a rental car can navigate. Here you’ll find quiet coves, picnic areas and the chance to spot migratory birds, adding an educational twist to a beach day that the whole family can enjoy.

sections.title: Birdwatching and Secluded Coves in the Kunë‑Vain‑Tale Reserve
sections.body: The Kunë‑Vain‑Tale Reserve, Albania’s first protected area, stretches along the Lezhë coast and offers more than just sand. With a car you can explore its network of unpaved paths leading to isolated beaches where the Adriatic meets wetlands. Early mornings bring flocks of storks, herons and rare shorebirds, making it a hotspot for birdwatchers. Parking areas are limited, so arriving early ensures you can park close to the water and walk to the most tranquil spots without a long trek.

tip.title: Pro Tip: Leave Early to Beat the Heat
tip.body: Pick up your car at AutoConnect Rental before 9 am, then head straight to the northern coast. The morning light keeps the sand cool, the roads are less busy, and you’ll have the whole day to explore without rushing back to Tirana.

faq.question: Are there sandy beaches in Northern Albania?
faq.answer: Yes. The northern stretch features primarily sandy beaches such as Shëngjin, Velipojë and several secluded bays within the Kunë‑Vain‑Tale Reserve, all offering fine, soft sand and shallow waters.

faq.question: How do you get to Rana e Hedhun without a car?
faq.answer: Rana e Hedhun is not served by public transport. The only practical ways to reach the dune are by private car, taxi or a pre‑arranged shuttle from nearby towns.

faq.question: Which is better for families, Northern or Southern Albania beaches?
faq.answer: Northern beaches tend to be sandier and have gentler, shallower water, making them more suitable for children. Southern beaches are often rocky coves, which can be beautiful but less ideal for family swimming and sand play.
